note: Targets that fill the gap-analysis bars. GROUND = wire one external authoritative source per empirical domain (each done = the ground bar rises live). ORIGINAL_TEXT = the Scripture-onboard milestones (each done = that bar rises). Mark status 'done' as completed; gap_analysis.py reads this and recomputes the bars. The Word/reasoning domains (theology, philosophy, rhetoric, witness, giving) are NOT here -- they ground through the original-text + corpus build, not external data.

original_text.name: Hebrew OT original + Strong's + morphology onboard
Greek NT original + Strong's + morphology onboard
read_passage tool: original + translation + lexical takes
lexicon takes wired (Strong's, then BDB / Thayer)
commentary takes searchable per verse (CCEL: Henry, Barnes, Calvin)
original_text.acquire: Great-minds commentary is NOT clean verse-keyed JSON anywhere quick (scouted 2026-06-15: bolls.life exposes no commentary list, only 3 lexicons; godlytalias/scrollmapper carry translations only). Authoritative public-domain home = CCEL ThML (ccel.org: Barnes' Notes is the most verse-by-verse; Henry/Calvin are passage-level) OR CrossWire Sword modules (zText, needs pysword). Plan: dedicated tick -- parse one commentary (Barnes first) to a verse/passage-keyed lw/00_source/commentary/*.db, wire commentary(reference) tool returning the attributed note per verse, takes-style. Verdict ATTRIBUTED Layer-2 take, never engine doctrine.

original_text.name: sermon takes searchable (Spurgeon, Edwards, Wesley)
original_text.acquire: Public-domain sermons (Spurgeon's ~3500 NPSP/MTP, Edwards, Wesley) are prose, not verse-keyed. Source: CCEL + Spurgeon Center / archive.org plaintext. Plan: dedicated tick -- index by primary_scripture (each sermon already cites a text), build lw/00_source/sermons/*.db keyed verse->sermon, wire sermon_take(reference). Local data/sermons/sermons.jsonl holds only 3 of Matt's own outlines (not the public-domain corpus). ATTRIBUTED Layer-2.

original_text.name: BDB/Thayer richer lexical take layered onto read_passage
original_text.acquire: CONFIRMED clean source (2026-06-15): bolls.life GET /dictionary-definition/BDBT/<G|H strongs>/ returns Brown-Driver-Briggs (Hebrew) + Thayer (Greek) as JSON, Strong's-keyed. Bulk = STEPBible TBESH/TBESG TSV (CC BY) to avoid ~14k API calls. Plan: build lw/00_source/lexicon_bdbt/*.db, add as a SECOND attributed take in read_passage beside Strong's. This is the named 'then BDB/Thayer' depth from the lexicon milestone.
